enrichment.short_description: Developer security platform that scans code, open source dependencies, containers, and IaC for vulnerabilities within IDEs and CI/CD pipelines.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Snyk scans code, open source dependencies, containers, and infrastructure-as-code for vulnerabilities directly in your IDE and CI/CD pipeline.

features.title: Snyk Code (SAST)
features.description: Static analysis that finds security vulnerabilities in application source code.

features.title: Snyk Open Source (SCA)
features.description: Scans open source dependencies for known vulnerabilities and license risk.

features.title: Snyk Container
features.description: Scans container images for vulnerable base images and packages.

features.title: Snyk IaC
features.description: Checks infrastructure-as-code configurations for misconfigurations.

features.title: IDE and CI/CD integrations
features.description: Integrates with IDEs, source code managers, and CI/CD pipelines including AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex.

pros: Free tier available with no credit card required
Covers code, dependencies, containers, and IaC from one platform
Integrates directly into developer IDEs and CI/CD workflows
Supports regional data hosting (US, EU, AU)
cons: Free plan is capped at 5 projects and limited monthly test counts
Enterprise-level features (custom rules, full SDLC automation) require the top-tier or custom-priced Enterprise plan
Per-developer pricing can scale up quickly for larger teams

faqs.question: Does Snyk have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snyk offers a Free tier for up to 5 projects with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What does the Team plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Team plan starts at $25/month per contributing developer, billed monthly.

faqs.question: Does Snyk scan containers and infrastructure code?
faqs.answer: Yes, in addition to code and open source dependency scanning, Snyk offers Snyk Container and Snyk IaC products.

faqs.question: Can Snyk integrate with AI coding tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snyk integrates with AI coding assistants including Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex.

use_cases.title: Securing open source dependencies
use_cases.description: Development teams scan third-party packages for known vulnerabilities before shipping.

use_cases.title: Shift-left code security
use_cases.description: Security teams embed SAST scanning into IDEs and pull requests to catch issues early.

use_cases.title: Container and IaC hardening
use_cases.description: Platform teams check container images and infrastructure-as-code templates for misconfigurations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Snyk?
paragraphs: Snyk is a developer security platform that scans application code, open source dependencies, containers, and infrastructure-as-code for vulnerabilities. It integrates into IDEs, source code managers, and CI/CD pipelines so issues can be caught before deployment.
The platform also connects with AI coding assistants such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex to validate AI-generated code as it is written.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Snyk is for
paragraphs: Snyk is aimed at development and AppSec teams that need vulnerability scanning across the full software stack, from a single free-tier developer up to enterprise organizations needing custom security rules and dedicated support.

enrichment.short_description: Static analysis security platform combining SAST, software composition analysis, and secrets detection with AI-assisted triage.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Semgrep combines SAST, open source dependency scanning, and secrets detection with AI-powered triage across 35+ programming languages.

features.title: Code (SAST)
features.description: Static analysis with a Pro Engine and Pro Rules to detect vulnerabilities like IDORs and business logic flaws.

features.title: Supply Chain (SCA)
features.description: Reachability analysis prioritizes exploitable open source dependencies, reducing false positives.

features.title: Semantic secrets detection
features.description: Detects and blocks hardcoded credentials before code is merged.

features.title: AI-powered triage memory
features.description: Learns from past triage decisions to automatically suppress repeat false positives.

features.title: Broad ecosystem integration
features.description: Works with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure; supports VS Code and JetBrains IDEs, plus an MCP server for AI tools like Cursor and Replit.

pros: Free Community Edition covers up to 10 contributors and 10 repositories
Supports 35+ programming languages
Reachability analysis reduces false positives in dependency findings
Includes secrets, SAST, and supply chain scanning in one product line
cons: Secrets detection is a paid $15/month add-on on the Teams plan
Free tier is capped at 10 contributors and 10 repositories
Enterprise pricing and on-prem SCM support require custom/quote-based plans

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Semgrep?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free Edition supports up to 10 contributors and 10 repositories with Code and Supply Chain scanning included.

faqs.question: How much does the Teams plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Teams plan starts at $30/month per contributor, billed monthly, covering up to 500 private repositories.

faqs.question: Does Semgrep detect secrets?
faqs.answer: Yes, secrets detection is available, though on the Teams plan it is a $15/month add-on.

faqs.question: How many languages does Semgrep support?
faqs.answer: The platform supports 35+ programming languages.

use_cases.title: Static code scanning in CI/CD
use_cases.description: Developers run SAST scans on pull requests via GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or Azure integrations.

use_cases.title: Dependency risk reduction
use_cases.description: AppSec teams use reachability analysis to prioritize truly exploitable open source vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Secrets prevention
use_cases.description: Teams add semantic secrets detection to block hardcoded credentials before merge.

features: Code (SAST) with Pro Engine and Pro Rules

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Semgrep?
paragraphs: Semgrep is an application security platform that combines static analysis (SAST), software composition analysis (SCA), and secrets detection to find and help remediate vulnerabilities before deployment.
It uses a mix of rule-based analysis and AI reasoning, including reachability analysis for dependencies and a triage memory that suppresses repeat false positives.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Semgrep is for
paragraphs: Semgrep serves developers who want fast, integrated security feedback as well as AppSec teams and CISOs who need centralized visibility across code and dependency risk.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based code quality and security platform that scans code for bugs, vulnerabilities, and code smells across 40+ languages.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SonarQube Cloud scans code in real time for bugs, vulnerabilities, and code smells across 40+ languages, with CI/CD and IDE integrations.

features.title: Automated code review
features.description: Integrates with CI/CD pipelines to provide pull request feedback and enforce quality gates.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Covers 40+ programming languages including Java, Python, JavaScript, C#, and infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform and Kubernetes.

features.title: AI CodeFix
features.description: Suggests one-click AI-assisted fixes for detected issues.

features.title: Secrets detection
features.description: Identifies exposed credentials and other sensitive data in code.

features.title: Test coverage tracking
features.description: Measures how much code is exercised by automated tests.

features.title: Compliance reporting
features.description: Supports NIST SSDF, OWASP, CWE, STIG, and PCI DSS reporting standards.

pros: Free tier available for individual developers
Covers 40+ languages including IaC tools like Terraform and Kubernetes
14-day free trial for paid features
Integrates with GitHub, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab, and Azure DevOps
cons: Team plan pricing recently increased (from $65 to $32/month per the vendor's own before/after framing, indicating a prior higher price point)
Enterprise plan requires custom annual pricing
Full platform value depends on CI/CD integration setup

faqs.question: Is SonarQube Cloud free?
faqs.answer: A free tier is available for individual developers; paid Team plans start at $32/month.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is offered for paid features.

faqs.question: Which languages does it support?
faqs.answer: SonarQube Cloud supports 40+ programming languages plus infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform and Kubernetes.

faqs.question: Does it integrate with my Git provider?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with GitHub, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab, and Azure DevOps.

use_cases.title: Pull request quality gates
use_cases.description: Teams enforce code quality and security standards automatically on every pull request.

use_cases.title: AI-generated code verification
use_cases.description: Teams scan AI-generated and developer-written code together in real time.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams adopting AI coding tools

use_cases.title: Compliance reporting
use_cases.description: Organizations generate reports aligned to NIST SSDF, OWASP, and PCI DSS.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SonarQube Cloud?
paragraphs: SonarQube Cloud (formerly SonarCloud) is a cloud-based code quality and security platform that scans source code in real time to find bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells, and architectural issues.
It integrates with CI/CD pipelines and Git providers to give pull request feedback and enforce quality gates before code ships, and is used by over 7 million developers according to the vendor.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SonarQube Cloud is for
paragraphs: It serves individual developers and small teams on the free tier, as well as enterprise organizations in healthcare, financial services, retail, and government that need compliance reporting and custom annual plans.

enrichment.short_description: Secrets detection and non-human identity governance platform that finds and remediates exposed credentials in code and CI/CD.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GitGuardian detects and helps remediate exposed secrets and credentials across code repositories, CI/CD, and developer machines.

features.title: Internal secrets monitoring
features.description: Scans code repositories, CI/CD pipelines, and container registries for exposed credentials.

features.title: Public secrets monitoring
features.description: Monitors public GitHub for exposed credentials in real time.

features.title: Non-Human Identity (NHI) governance
features.description: Discovers and manages service accounts, API keys, and OAuth tokens.

features.title: Developer endpoint protection
features.description: Detects credentials present on developer laptops.

features.title: GitGuardian CLI (ggshield)
features.description: Detects 550+ secret types directly from developer tools.

pros: Free Starter plan for up to 25 developers with no credit card required
Detects 550+ secret types via its ggshield CLI
Multi-VCS support including GitHub, GitLab, Azure Repos, and Bitbucket
Ranked as a top security app on the GitHub Marketplace by the vendor
cons: Pull request scanning on the free plan is limited to GitHub only
Business and Enterprise pricing is quote-based rather than published
Public Secrets Monitoring and NHI governance features are reserved for the Enterprise plan

faqs.question: Does GitGuardian have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free for up to 25 developers with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How is Business/Enterprise pricing structured?
faqs.answer: Both the Business (Teams) and Enterprise plans use custom, quote-based pricing.

faqs.question: What does ggshield detect?
faqs.answer: GitGuardian's CLI, ggshield, detects 550+ types of secrets across development tools.

faqs.question: Which version control systems are supported?
faqs.answer: GitGuardian supports GitHub, GitLab, Azure Repos, and Bitbucket.

use_cases.title: Preventing secret leaks in commits
use_cases.description: Developers use git hooks and IDE extensions to catch hardcoded credentials before pushing code.

use_cases.title: Enterprise secrets governance
use_cases.description: Security teams centrally monitor and remediate exposed secrets and manage non-human identities.

use_cases.title: Public exposure monitoring
use_cases.description: Organizations track whether their credentials have leaked onto public GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GitGuardian?
paragraphs: GitGuardian is a secrets security and non-human identity governance platform that finds, prevents, and remediates exposed credentials across the development lifecycle, from developer laptops to CI/CD pipelines and public GitHub.
Its ggshield CLI detects 550+ types of secrets, and the platform offers pre-commit and pre-push checks alongside centralized incident monitoring.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GitGuardian is for
paragraphs: It serves individual developers on the free Starter plan up to large enterprises needing public exposure monitoring, self-hosted deployment, and non-human identity governance.

enrichment.short_description: Supply chain security tool that scans open source dependencies for malicious behavior and blocks risky packages before install.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Socket scans open source dependencies for malicious behavior, detecting 70+ risk types and blocking risky packages before they ship.

features.title: Risk type detection
features.description: Detects 70+ risk types in open source dependencies.

features.title: Automatic blocking
features.description: Automatically blocks malicious dependencies from entering a project.

features.title: AI-powered behavior analysis
features.description: Uses AI to analyze packages for hidden malicious behaviors.

features.title: Reachability analysis
features.description: Precomputed reachability analysis cuts CVE false positives, per the vendor, by up to 60% on paid plans.

features.title: SBOM import/export
features.description: Supports software bill of materials import and export on the Business plan.

pros: Free plan includes unlimited developers and repos with 1,000 scans/month
Detects 70+ categories of dependency risk, not just known CVEs
Reachability analysis reduces false positive CVE alerts
Business plan adds SSO/SAML and compliance integrations (e.g. Vanta)
cons: Free plan is capped to 3 members and 1 repository label
Unlimited scans and API quota require the Business plan
Enterprise-level multi-platform VCS support (GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps) requires custom pricing

faqs.question: Is Socket free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan offers unlimited developers and repos with 1,000 scans per month.

faqs.question: What does the Team plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Team plan starts at $25/month per developer, with a 20% discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: Does Socket support GitLab or Bitbucket?
faqs.answer: Multi-platform VCS support for GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps is available on the Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: Does Socket integrate with Slack?
faqs.answer: Yes, Slack integration for security alerts is available starting on the Team plan.

use_cases.title: Blocking malicious packages
use_cases.description: Teams automatically block newly published malicious open source packages before install.

use_cases.title: Reducing CVE alert noise
use_cases.description: Security teams use reachability analysis to focus only on exploitable dependency vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Compliance-ready supply chain security
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use SBOM export and SSO/SAML to meet compliance requirements.

features: Multi-platform VCS support (GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Socket?
paragraphs: Socket is a supply chain security tool that scans open source dependencies for malicious or risky behavior, detecting 70+ risk types and automatically blocking dangerous packages before they enter a codebase.
It uses AI-powered analysis to flag hidden malicious behaviors and offers reachability analysis to reduce false-positive CVE alerts on paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Socket is for
paragraphs: Socket is used by development teams wanting free, unlimited-developer dependency scanning, as well as larger organizations that need SBOM export, SSO/SAML, and compliance integrations on paid plans.

enrichment.short_description: Unified application security platform covering code, cloud, and runtime scanning plus AI-powered pentesting from one dashboard.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Aikido Security unifies SAST, SCA, secrets detection, cloud posture management, and runtime protection in one developer-focused platform.

features.title: Code security (SAST/SCA)
features.description: Static code analysis, open source dependency scanning, and secrets detection.

features.title: Cloud Security Posture Management
features.description: Scans cloud accounts, virtual machines, and Kubernetes/container configurations.

features.title: AI-powered pentesting
features.description: Runs automated penetration tests using AI agents.

features.title: Zen runtime protection
features.description: Runtime firewall that blocks bot and injection attacks in production.

features.title: AutoTriage alert deduplication
features.description: Uses contextual analysis and custom rules to reduce alert noise.

pros: Free forever plan with no credit card required
Combines code, cloud, and runtime security in a single platform
Offers AI AutoFixes for detected issues on paid plans
Separate transparent pricing published for one-off and continuous pentesting
cons: Free plan is limited to 2 users, 10 repos, and 1 cloud account
On-prem scanning and REST API fuzzing require the Pro plan or higher
Enterprise features require custom, quote-based pricing

faqs.question: Is Aikido Security free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Developer plan is free forever for 2 users with limited repos, containers, and cloud accounts.

faqs.question: What does the Basic plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Basic plan starts at $300/month for 10 users, billed monthly or annually with a 10% discount.

faqs.question: Where is Aikido headquartered?
faqs.answer: Aikido Security is headquartered in Ghent, Belgium, with an additional office in San Francisco.

faqs.question: Does Aikido offer pentesting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aikido offers Standard, Rightsized, and Continuous pentest options priced separately from the platform plans.

use_cases.title: Consolidating AppSec tooling
use_cases.description: Development teams replace multiple point security tools with one unified platform.

use_cases.title: Cloud misconfiguration detection
use_cases.description: Teams scan cloud accounts and Kubernetes clusters for posture issues.

use_cases.title: On-demand penetration testing
use_cases.description: Organizations purchase scoped or continuous pentests alongside automated scanning.

features: 10 repos, 2 container images, 1 domain, 1 cloud account
100 repos, 25 container images, 3 domains, 3 cloud accounts
200 repos, 50 container images, 10 domains, 10 cloud accounts
500 repos, 100 container images, 20 domains, 20 cloud accounts

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Aikido Security?
paragraphs: Aikido Security is an application security platform that unifies code scanning (SAST, SCA, secrets), cloud security posture management, and runtime protection into a single dashboard.
Founded in 2022 and based in Ghent, Belgium, it also offers AI-powered automated penetration testing and a runtime firewall (Zen) to block attacks in production.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Aikido Security is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at development teams that want to consolidate multiple security tools into one platform, from small teams on the free plan to organizations needing on-prem scanning and dedicated pentesting engagements.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud security platform unifying code, cloud infrastructure, and runtime protection for enterprise security teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Wiz is a cloud security platform connecting code, cloud infrastructure, and runtime protection with AI-driven automation.

features.title: Wiz Code
features.description: Secures development from the IDE through CI/CD deployment.

features.title: Wiz Cloud
features.description: Protects cloud infrastructure and AI applications.

features.title: Wiz Defend
features.description: Provides runtime protection and threat detection.

features.title: AI security agents
features.description: Automates code fixes, penetration testing, and threat investigations.

pros: Unifies code, cloud, and runtime security in one platform
Supports multi-cloud environments including AWS and Azure
Offers a free trial before purchase
Used by a large share of Fortune 100 companies according to the vendor
cons: Full platform access requires quote-based custom pricing rather than published rates
No self-serve/free-forever tier is advertised on the site
Primarily positioned for enterprise-scale organizations

faqs.question: Does Wiz offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Wiz offers a free trial with demo scheduling available on their website.

faqs.question: Is Wiz pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, full platform pricing is quote-based; a demo or sales conversation is required.

faqs.question: Which cloud providers does Wiz support?
faqs.answer: Wiz supports multi-cloud environments including AWS and Azure.

faqs.question: What are the three main components of Wiz?
faqs.answer: Wiz Code, Wiz Cloud, and Wiz Defend cover development, cloud infrastructure, and runtime protection respectively.

use_cases.title: Unified cloud risk visibility
use_cases.description: Security teams get a single view of vulnerabilities and misconfigurations across cloud environments.

use_cases.title: Shift-left code security
use_cases.description: Developers get security feedback from the IDE through CI/CD before deployment.

use_cases.title: Runtime threat detection
use_cases.description: Organizations use Wiz Defend to detect and investigate active threats in running workloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Wiz?
paragraphs: Wiz is a cloud security platform that connects code, cloud infrastructure, and runtime protection into one system, spanning development through production for multi-cloud environments including AWS and Azure.
The platform uses AI agents to automate parts of security operations, such as generating code fixes and conducting threat investigations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Wiz is for
paragraphs: Wiz targets enterprise security teams managing large, multi-cloud environments, with reported adoption among a majority of Fortune 100 companies.

enrichment.short_description: Agentless cloud-native application protection platform (CNAPP) covering vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and malware from code to runtime.
enrichment.headquarters: London, Tel Aviv, and Portland, OR
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Orca Security is an agentless CNAPP that scans cloud environments for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and malware without installing agents.

features.title: Agentless scanning
features.description: Delivers full cloud visibility within 24 hours without installing agents.

features.title: Reachability analysis
features.description: Analyzes risk across agentless, dynamic, and code-level layers to reduce alert noise.

features.title: AI-powered capabilities
features.description: Includes automated code fixes, risk assessment, and an AI Assistant.

features.title: Shift-left developer integration
features.description: Integrates into CI/CD pipelines and IDEs for pre-deployment security checks.

features.title: Unified Data Model
features.description: Combines context across misconfigurations, vulnerabilities, identity risks, and sensitive data.

pros: Agentless deployment claims full visibility within 24 hours
Combines vulnerability, misconfiguration, and malware detection in one platform
Integrates with 25+ tools including AWS, GitHub, Slack, Jira, and Okta
Founded in 2019 with offices across three regions (London, Tel Aviv, Portland)
cons: No published self-serve pricing; requires a sales conversation
Primarily targeted at enterprise organizations rather than solo developers
ROI/savings figures cited by the vendor are not independently broken down by plan tier

faqs.question: Does Orca require installing agents?
faqs.answer: No, Orca uses agentless scanning to achieve visibility, according to the vendor, within 24 hours.

faqs.question: When was Orca Security founded?
faqs.answer: Orca Security was founded in 2019.

faqs.question: Where is Orca headquartered?
faqs.answer: Orca operates from offices in London, Tel Aviv, and Portland, Oregon.

faqs.question: Is Orca pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, specific pricing is not published on the site.

use_cases.title: Agentless cloud risk visibility
use_cases.description: Security teams get full visibility into cloud misconfigurations and vulnerabilities without deploying agents.

use_cases.title: Reducing alert fatigue
use_cases.description: Teams use multi-layer reachability analysis to focus on truly exploitable risks.

use_cases.title: Shift-left cloud security
use_cases.description: Developers integrate cloud security checks into CI/CD pipelines and IDEs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Orca Security?
paragraphs: Orca Security is a Cloud-Native Application Protection Platform (CNAPP) that provides agentless scanning for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, malware, and identity risks across cloud environments.
Founded in 2019 and operating from offices in London, Tel Aviv, and Portland, Oregon, Orca combines agentless, dynamic, and code-level reachability analysis to reduce alert noise.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Orca Security is for
paragraphs: Orca serves enterprise organizations building cloud-native applications, from Fortune 500 companies to cloud-native startups, that need unified visibility across their cloud estate.

name: Lacework
skip_reason: The official lacework.com domain now 301-redirects to fortinet.com/products/forticnapp. Lacework was acquired and its cloud security technology has been folded into Fortinet's FortiCNAPP product; there is no longer an independent Lacework website or product page to research, so the product does not verifiably exist under the name 'Lacework' anymore.

enrichment.short_description: Exposure management platform that identifies vulnerabilities across IT, cloud, identity, OT, and AI attack surfaces, anchored by Tenable One.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tenable's exposure management platform, led by Tenable One and Nessus, identifies vulnerabilities across IT, cloud, identity, and OT environments.

features.title: Tenable One
features.description: AI-powered exposure management platform providing unified visibility across infrastructure, cloud, identity, OT, and AI environments.

features.title: Hexa AI
features.description: Agentic orchestration system for automated remediation.

features.title: Nessus
features.description: Vulnerability scanner available in Professional and Expert editions.

features.title: Cloud Security/CNAPP
features.description: Cloud-native application protection capabilities.

features.title: OT Security
features.description: Security monitoring for operational technology environments.

features.title: Web App Scanning and Patch Management
features.description: Additional specialized modules for web application and patch risk management.

pros: Broad coverage spanning IT, cloud, identity, OT, and AI attack surfaces
Nessus is a widely used, dedicated vulnerability scanner product
Published starting prices exist for some products (e.g. Tenable One VM, Nessus editions)
Named a leader by Gartner, IDC, and Forrester in 2025 per the vendor
cons: Full Tenable One platform pricing beyond the base tier is not fully itemized on the public site
Multiple separate product lines (Nessus, Tenable One, OT Security, etc.) can add complexity when choosing a package
Headquarters location is not stated on the pages reviewed

faqs.question: What is Tenable One?
faqs.answer: Tenable One is the company's flagship AI-powered exposure management platform, unifying visibility across infrastructure, cloud, identity, OT, and AI environments.

faqs.question: How much does Nessus cost?
faqs.answer: Nessus Professional is priced at $4,790/year and Nessus Expert at $6,790/year.

faqs.question: When was Tenable founded?
faqs.answer: Tenable was founded in 2002.

faqs.question: Does Tenable offer multi-year pricing discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes, multi-year discounts are available according to the vendor.

use_cases.title: Enterprise vulnerability management
use_cases.description: Security teams use Tenable One for unified exposure visibility across their attack surface.

use_cases.title: Standalone vulnerability scanning
use_cases.description: Teams use Nessus Professional or Expert for dedicated vulnerability scans.

use_cases.title: OT/industrial security
use_cases.description: Organizations with operational technology environments monitor OT-specific risks.

features: Unified visibility across infrastructure, cloud, identity, OT, and AI
Expanded scanning capabilities beyond Professional edition

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tenable?
paragraphs: Tenable is a cybersecurity company specializing in exposure management, helping organizations identify and close gaps between vulnerability detection and actual exploitation across their attack surface.

Its flagship product, Tenable One, unifies visibility across IT infrastructure, cloud, identity, operational technology (OT), and AI environments, alongside standalone products like the Nessus vulnerability scanner.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tenable is for
paragraphs: Founded in 2002, Tenable serves enterprise security teams and CISOs across sectors including financial services, healthcare, energy, and government who need to manage vulnerabilities across complex, hybrid environments.
